meta.originalQuestion: How should we restructure Judge Gym V3 analysis so we can explore a large experiment matrix systematically, triage unusable figures, choose appropriate aggregation methods beyond or alongside DST/TBM, and support subagent-assisted iterative analysis from one stable cached data source before writing the final V3 report?

scores.component: Frozen analysis contract + isolated subagent roles
Aggregation stack: geometry + weighted pooling primary; DST/TBM diagnostic

areasOfAnalysis.title: Report Architecture and Figure Triage
questions: How should the current V3 analysis output be partitioned into exploratory artifacts versus report-grade figures?
Which existing figure types are likely to be unreadable or misleading because of scale mixing, overplotting, or oversized matrices?
What minimal chart set should anchor the final V3 report?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Statistical Exploration and Derived Tables
questions: Which additional derived tables and statistical summaries would let us mine the V3 matrix more systematically than browsing PNGs?
How should we prioritize matched contrasts, sample instability, uncertainty, and evidence-group analyses?
What exploration outputs should be generated automatically before report writing?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Aggregation Alternatives Beyond DST/TBM
questions: When is DST/TBM still informative in this analysis and when should it be demoted to a diagnostic lens?
Which alternative aggregation methods are most appropriate for set-valued, confidence-weighted judgments over many dependent sources?
Should aggregation be local per evidence group or global per sample, and how should that affect the report?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Subagent-Guided Analysis Process and Guardrails
questions: How should we structure subagent work so multiple analysis passes can operate on the same cached data source without drifting?
What guardrails should govern figure generation, spot-checking, and hypothesis mining?
What execution sequence should we follow before freezing the final V3 report?
